Hey, welcome back and welcome to the module on looping, which is the strategy or more accurately the secret sauce I created for the back half of the straight line system. Essentially, what looping allows you to do is take a prospect who's sitting on the fence after you've made your initial pitch, meaning you've asked for the order and they've come back to you with something along the lines of they want to think about it or call you back or talk to their wife or one of the other dozen typical objections that we always hear and you continue to move them down the straight line from the open to the close in a way that doesn't break rapport with them and hence blow the sale. Typically, that's what happens during this so-called objection handling phase of the sale. A salesman tends to break rapport with his prospect, but through the process of looping, a salesman can stay in tight rapport with their prospect while answering all of their objections as they simultaneously continue to move the prospect down the straight line from the open to the close. Essentially, it's that point in the sale we've already been through your initial qualifying pitch, which means you should be in tight rapport with your prospect already, both on a conscious and an unconscious level, right? Then you've used that tight rapport to gather intelligence, which includes identifying needs and also finding out where their pain lies and amplifying it if necessary. And remember, you didn't try to solve their pain at this point. You left the wound open or maybe even dug into it a bit. So be that much more open to hearing about your solution. Then you laid out that solution for them by the main body of your script, which you kept short and sweet, right? You didn't front load your benefits. You simply laid out the basics of a very compelling solution, kept the bulk of your powder dry. Then you ended the pitch with some urgency and then calmly asked for your order using a simple yet extremely effective language pattern from the ones I gave you. Something like, and believe me, John, if you do even half as well as the rest of my clients in this program, the only problem you'll have is that you didn't buy more. Sound fair enough? And of course, those last few words, right? Use the reasonable man tone. So it sounded like sound fair enough, not like you're daring the guy to disagree, like sound fair enough, huh, huh? Go ahead, make my day. That's not what you're doing. It's calm, cool, collected, the reasonable man tone. So basically, you were smooth as silk, almost to the point where your tonality was so calm and so reasonable that only a complete moron with no social grace would be rude enough to say no to you. And that's exactly what your goal is here, is to make it so easy for him and so low pressure that he feels almost compelled to cave in right there on the spot and give you the order the very first time you ask for it. Especially that perfect mix of just the right words, just the right tone out to literally ease someone down the straight line to a completely objectionless close where they just say yes right out of the gate. Now it would be great if sales actually worked that way. I mean, you could actually execute the front half of the sale perfectly and eliminate the need for the back half of the sale, right? But it just doesn't work that way. I mean, no matter how great you sound, no matter how on target your words were, the prospect is still going to hit you with some sort of objection. Even if he's forced to conjure up a completely bogus one, you see, it's very, very rare that a prospect will come straight out and tell you what's truly holding them back. And of course we know what it is from the earlier modules, right? What's holding them back is either they're not certain enough about one of the three tens or perhaps they have a very, very high action threshold that's stopping them from taking action even though they know they should be taking action, right? Instead they'll come up with objections and then more objections and more objections. He'll say he still needs to think about it or call back or he's got to do some research or talk with his wife or his attorney or his accountant or wait for a better time of year like after tax time or it's closer to ground hours they are when Santa Claus or the Tooth Fairy is giving them the all clear sign. You get my point? When you ask for the order that first time, you have to be prepared to handle objection after objection after objection. You get it? Now the danger here is that this is the spot where a typical salesman ends up plunging into the so-called death spiral. It starts with the prospect giving him a bogus objection to which the salesman spits back a canned response designed to overcome that objection. Then the salesman asks for the order again to which the prospect who just got an answer to a question that he really didn't even care about in the first place was a bogus objection, right? He has no choice now but to give the salesman yet another bogus objection to which the salesman then spits out yet another canned response and asks for the order again which then forces the prospect to come up with yet another bogus objection and on and on we go. The sort of death spiral where the prospect isn't being honest with you. He's just, you know, he's saying he's got to talk to his wife or he's got to do this, he's got to do that. That's not what's holding him back. What's holding him back is that he's just not certain enough yet. It could be about the product, it could be about you, it could be about your company, it could be as a very high action threshold, maybe he's in denial of his pain, but what's holding him back is not that he actually has to think about it. That's the stolt, the smoke screen. It's what lies underneath. That's where the magic is that allows you to close the sale. And this process of looping, what it's going to do is going to show you how to quickly and elegantly essentially get past those initial objections and seamlessly transition into the very heart of what holds someone back from making a positive buying decision. And that my friend is certainty. Looping will show you how to build absolute certainty in your client's heart, in their mind, both logically and emotionally. And at the same time, you're moving them down the straight line from the open to the close until you've gotten to that point where they're so certain, essentially that their certainties exceeded their action threshold and bam, everybody starts to buy. That's the process of looping.
